summary: In his 1795 essay 'Perpetual Peace', Immanuel Kant outlines a structured political program to eliminate war. He argues that lasting peace requires the abolition of standing armies and secret treaties, the establishment of republican governments, a federation of free states, and the guarantee of universal hospitality for global citizens.

principles.statement: The civil constitution of every state must be republican.
principles.rationale: Republican governments (defined by representative government and separation of legislature from executive) tend towards pacifism more than other forms of government, making them a necessary foundation for peace.
principles.source_quote: The civil constitution of each state shall be republican.

principles.statement: International order should be based on a federation of free states, not a single global government.
principles.rationale: A league of independent republican states solves the anarchy of the international system without risking the tyranny that a single world government would inevitably produce.
principles.source_quote: The law of nations shall be founded on a federation of free states.

principles.statement: Global citizens possess a right to universal hospitality.
principles.rationale: Strangers have the right to visit foreign lands without facing hostility (provided they have no malintent), which is necessary to accomplish intercommunication and peaceful relations between nations.
principles.source_quote: The rights of men, as citizens of the world, shall be limited to the conditions of universal hospitality.

principles.statement: Standing armies must eventually be dismantled.
principles.rationale: Maintaining standing armies creates external friction between states and inherently threatens peace.
principles.source_quote: Standing armies shall in time be totally abolished

frameworks.name: The Perpetual Peace Program
frameworks.when_to_apply: When governments seek to transition from a state of international hostility to a permanent, structural peace.
steps: Implement the 'Preliminary Articles' immediately or with deliberate speed (e.g., abolish secret treaties, standing armies, and national debts for war; prohibit buying/exchanging states and interfering in foreign governments).
Establish the 'Definitive Articles' to build a foundation for peace: ensure all states have republican constitutions.
Form a federation of these free, republican states.
Guarantee the rights of universal hospitality to allow peaceful intercommunication between nations.
frameworks.source_quote: Kant proposed a peace program to be implemented by governments. The "Preliminary Articles" described these steps that should be taken immediately... Three Definitive Articles would provide not merely a cessation of hostilities but a foundation on which to build a peace.

mental_models.name: Federation vs. World Government
mental_models.description: A lens for structuring international relations that balances the need for global order with the dangers of centralized power. Instead of a single, monolithic world state (which trends toward tyranny), order is achieved through a voluntary league or confederation of independent, republican states.
mental_models.example: Kant argued against a world government, arguing it would be prone to tyranny. The preferable solution to anarchy in the international system was to create a league of independent republican states.

heuristics: No state shall by force interfere with the constitution or government of another state
No secret treaty of peace shall be held valid in which there is tacitly reserved matter for a future war
National debts shall not be contracted with a view to the external friction of states

signature_quotes.text: The civil constitution of each state shall be republican.
signature_quotes.context: The first Definitive Article for building a foundation of peace.

signature_quotes.text: The law of nations shall be founded on a federation of free states.
signature_quotes.context: The second Definitive Article for perpetual peace.

signature_quotes.text: The rights of men, as citizens of the world, shall be limited to the conditions of universal hospitality.
signature_quotes.context: The third Definitive Article, establishing cosmopolitan rights.

signature_quotes.text: Standing armies shall in time be totally abolished
signature_quotes.context: One of the Preliminary Articles for immediate implementation.

signature_quotes.text: No independent states, large or small, shall come under the dominion of another state by inheritance, exchange, purchase, or donation
signature_quotes.context: A Preliminary Article protecting the sovereignty of states.

anti_patterns.description: Establishing a single world government to enforce global peace.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It would be prone to tyranny, replacing international anarchy with global oppression.

anti_patterns.description: Engaging in dishonorable acts of hostility during a war, such as using assassins, poisoners, or inciting treason.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It destroys trust and makes mutual confidence in the subsequent peace impossible.

anti_patterns.description: Signing secret treaties that tacitly reserve matters for future conflicts.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It undermines the very definition of peace, treating it merely as a temporary truce rather than a perpetual resolution.
